statistics.madhhab_note: For scholars with a recorded madhhab only (about 9% of the total); not representative of the whole corpus.
statistics.genre_stream_note: The rise and decline of work genres across the centuries. Band thickness shows the number of works in that century.
statistics.havza_lang_flow: From basin to language: flow of works
statistics.havza_lang_note: Distribution by language of the works each basin produced. Ribbon thickness is proportional to the number of works (works with a recorded language).

about.title: About İTA
about.subtitle: A digital atlas and encyclopedia of the Islamic historiographical tradition
about.what_text: The Atlas of Islamic Historiography (İTA) is a website and encyclopedia project that introduces the contributions to historical writing made under the dynasties that ruled across thirteen regions of the Islamic world between the 1st–15th centuries AH (7th–21st centuries CE), examined through historians, works, historiographical genres, methods, and the historical concepts and terms they produced. It is the digital, visualized form of the Civilizational Basins History & Historiography (MHTT) research programme.
about.method_text: Focusing on the historical sources, historians, and historiographical processes of each region, the work proceeds through edited volumes, workshops, and symposia. Data shaped around the articles of period specialists has been de-duplicated, merged into a single database, and matched against the TDV Encyclopedia of Islam (DİA).
about.source_mhtt: Historiography in Muslim Regions — historiographical research covering Iran, Egypt, the Indian Subcontinent, and other regions.
about.source_dia: TDV Encyclopedia of Islam — 8,500+ biographical entries, teacher-student relations, geographic data, and importance scores.
about.team_macit_affil: Kocaeli University, Faculty of Theology, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_polatoglu_affil: İnönü University, Faculty of Theology, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_erol_affil: Kırklareli University, Faculty of Arts & Sciences, History Dept.
about.pillar_kronoloji_d: A timeline built around dynasties, historical events, and historians.
about.pillar_havzalar_d: The geographic regions that form the spatial ground of Islamic historiography.
about.pillar_hanedanlar_d: Dynasties contributing to historiography politically and culturally.
about.pillar_tarihciler_d: Authors renowned in the field of history.
about.pillar_kaynaklar_d: The historical works composed by these historians.
about.pillar_turler_d: The historiographical genres that shaped how sources were written.
about.pillar_metodoloji_d: The methods and principles that govern record-keeping.
about.pillar_kavramlar_d: The concepts and terms that make historiography possible.
about.aim_text: İTA aims to present the history of Muslim historiography as a coherent whole and to frame, develop, and disseminate Islamic historiography through mapping and data visualization. By examining prominent sources and historians across thirteen regions from the early conquests to the present, it seeks to reveal an integrated picture of historical writing and to offer a bibliographic resource that lets new researchers access sources easily and compare the dynamics, perspectives, and threads across periods.

insights.title: Structure of the Scholarly Network
insights.subtitle: Bridge figures and the longest teacher–student chains
insights.bridges_note: Scholars who most often act as “bridges” linking different scholarly circles, ranked by betweenness centrality. A high value marks a key transmitter channeling knowledge between distinct traditions.
insights.chains_note: Uninterrupted teacher→student chains found in the data. Linked names lead to İTA scholars; grey names are transmitters outside İTA. Longest unbroken chain
insights.havza_flow_note: Row = teacher’s basin, column = student’s basin. Cell intensity is proportional to the number of teacher→student ties in that direction. Covers only ties where both scholars are in İTA with a known basin
insights.havza_flow_legend: Diagonal = within-basin transmission; off-diagonal = across basins
insights.path_hint: You can find the shortest scholarly path between two specific scholars (who connects to whom, through whom) in the “path between two scholars” tool on the Silsile page.

health.title: Data Health
health.intro: A transparent breakdown of how fully each field is populated. This panel makes no new claim; it shows the coverage of the existing data and offers a roadmap for completion.
health.intro_short: Field coverage rates and a completion roadmap.
health.by_havza_note: City and death year are two key fields feeding the map and time analyses. The table shows which basin should be prioritised for completion.
health.downloads_note: The three core datasets, in CSV and JSON. Data is provided as-is; see the coverage sections above for missing fields.
health.dictionary_note: The meaning of each field. Fill rates are given in the sections above.
health.citation_note: Recommended citation when using this dataset or atlas:
health.doi_note: For a persistent, citable identifier, the dataset can be deposited on Zenodo; enabling the GitHub–Zenodo integration mints an automatic DOI for each release. Once a DOI is obtained, it can be added to this page.
health.license_note: The repository currently has no explicit license file. To clarify academic reuse and attribution, adding an open license for the data and content (e.g. CC BY 4.0) and a separate license for the code (e.g. MIT) is recommended.

canon.title: Influence and Canon
canon.subtitle: Scholars highlighted by importance score
canon.intro: A canon view organised by the importance score İTA assigns to each scholar. The rankings surface the leading names by period and by basin. The score is a composite, relative measure of prominence — not an absolute value.

milieus.title: Contemporary Milieus
milieus.subtitle: Clusters of scholars active in the same city and generation
milieus.intro: When scholars active in the same city and the same century are brought together, the contemporary milieus where scholarly production concentrated come into view. City–century clusters of at least three scholars are ranked by density.
milieus.note: Note: this view relies only on scholars with both a recorded city and death year

explorer.title: Power Explorer
explorer.subtitle: Filter basin, century, madhhab, genre and language at once
explorer.intro: Filter five dimensions at once; the result updates simultaneously as a timeline, a map and a list. Genre and language filters return scholars who authored works of that genre/language.

city.empty: No scholars associated with this city were found.
city.external_note: Search for this place name in external gazetteers and authority records.
